nodes.title: Model Links
widgets_values:
## LTX-2 Prompting Tips

1. **Core Actions**: Describe events and actions as they occur over time  
2. **Visual Details**: Describe all visual details you want to appear in the video  
3. **Audio**: Describe sounds and dialogue needed for the scene

## Report LTX-2 Issues
To report any issues when running this workflow, [go to GitHub](https://github.com/Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo/issues)


## Model Links (for Local Users)

**checkpoints**

- [ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-fp8.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Lightricks/LTX-2.3-fp8/resolve/main/ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-fp8.safetensors)

**text_encoders**

- [gemma_3_12B_it_fp4_mixed.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/ltx-2/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/gemma_3_12B_it_fp4_mixed.safetensors)


## Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 checkpoints/
│   │   └── ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-fp8.safetensors
│   └── 📂 text_encoders/
│       └── gemma_3_12B_it_fp4_mixed.safetensors
```

## Report Issue

Note: Please update ComfyUI first ([guide](https://docs.comfy.org/installation/update_comfyui)) and prepare required models. Desktop/Cloud updates follow stable releases, so some nightly-supported models may not be available yet.

- Cannot run / runtime errors: [ComfyUI/issues](https://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI/issues)
- UI / frontend issues: [ComfyUI_frontend/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/ComfyUI_frontend/issues)
- Workflow issues: [workflow_templates/issues](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/workflow_templates/issues)

The girl shifts her weight, lowering her sword to the grass, as the orange tabby stretches its front paws wide and lets out a tiny yawn, then bounds upward with a soft, chirping meow; she catches the cat in both arms, hugging it close as it nuzzles against her armored chest, the pom-pom on her helmet wobbling with her laugh—camera dollies in slowly, framing the pair as the cat purrs loudly and kneads its paws on her shoulder, the castle and mountains standing serene behind them.

The image shows a young girl dressed in medieval armor, standing in a field with a castle in the background. She is wearing a helmet with a pom-pom on top and has a sword in her right hand. The girl is standing with her left hand on her hip and her right arm resting on her thigh. In front of her, there is a orange tabby cat sitting on the grass. The cat is looking up at the girl with a curious expression. The field is surrounded by trees and bushes, and there are mountains in the distance. The sky is blue and the overall mood of the image is peaceful and serene.

The image is a digital illustration of a young girl dressed in medieval armor, holding a cat in her arms. The girl is wearing a helmet with a visor and has brown hair tied up in a ponytail. She is standing in a lush green landscape with a castle in the background. The cat is orange and is looking up at the girl with a curious expression. The sky is blue with white clouds and there are mountains and trees in the distance. The overall mood of the image is peaceful and serene.

blurry, out of focus, overexposed, underexposed, low contrast, washed out colors, excessive noise, grainy texture, poor lighting, flickering, motion blur, distorted proportions, unnatural skin tones, deformed facial features, asymmetrical face, missing facial features, extra limbs, disfigured hands, wrong hand count, artifacts around text, unreadable text on shirt or hat, incorrect lettering on cap (“PNTR”), incorrect t-shirt slogan (“JUST DO IT”), missing microphone, misplaced microphone, inconsistent perspective, camera shake, incorrect depth of field, background too sharp, background clutter, distracting reflections, harsh shadows, inconsistent lighting direction, color banding, cartoonish rendering, 3D CGI look, unrealistic materials, uncanny valley effect, incorrect ethnicity, wrong gender, exaggerated expressions, smiling, laughing, exaggerated sadness, wrong gaze direction, eyes looking at camera, mismatched lip sync, silent or muted audio, distorted voice, robotic voice, echo, background noise, off-sync audio, missing sniff sounds, incorrect dialogue, added dialogue, repetitive speech, jittery movement, awkward pauses, incorrect timing, unnatural transitions, inconsistent framing, tilted camera, missing door or shelves, missing shallow depth of field, flat lighting, inconsistent tone, cinematic oversaturation, stylized filters, or AI artifacts.
the bunny is happy while it displays his carrot

[META]
frozen: false
description: LTX-2.3 prompt rewriter that ingests vision-model descriptions of the input frame (and optionally the last frame) as guardrails, so it never writes motion or content that contradicts the frames already locked in. Outputs prompt only — no commentary, no fences.
thinking_mode: disabled

[SYSTEM]
You convert a natural-language user idea into ONE production-ready prompt for the LTX-2.3 video model. You are also given a vision-model description of the START frame (the input image), and optionally a description of the LAST frame. You emit the rewritten prompt and nothing else.

The frame descriptions are GUARDRAILS. The video model already has the actual frames locked in as conditioning — your job is only to make sure the prompt you write fits inside them. Use them to avoid proposing any subject, action, setting, or camera move that the frames cannot support. They are never copied into the output, and they are not a destination to steer toward — the motion comes from the user idea.

## OUTPUT CONTRACT
- Emit ONLY the rewritten prompt as a single flowing paragraph. No markdown, no fences, no quotes around the whole thing, no preamble, no labels, no notes.
- Write in present tense.
- Match length to duration: short prompts under-fill a clip. For 8–10s clips write a dense, multi-beat paragraph; for ~5s keep it tighter but still complete.
- Preserve non-ASCII characters as-is (accents, CJK, Cyrillic). Never escape or transliterate.

## HOW TO USE THE FRAME DESCRIPTIONS
- The START description tells you what is already on screen. Do NOT re-describe its static elements (appearance, clothing, setting, palette already visible). The model can see them. Write the motion that animates that starting state.
- The user idea is the source of the motion. The frame descriptions only BOUND it: reject or adjust any part of the idea that would require something the frames don't contain or that contradicts them.
- If a LAST-frame description is given, treat it as a boundary the motion must stay compatible with — do not write action that would end somewhere incompatible with it. Do NOT, however, narrate toward it or describe "arriving" at it; the model's latent conditioning handles the endpoint. Just don't contradict it.
- If the user idea conflicts with a frame, the frame wins: drop or rephrase the conflicting part so the prompt stays consistent with what's on screen.

## WHAT TO INCLUDE (motion-focused, folded into the paragraph)
1. ACTION — the core motion as one natural sequence, start to finish, beginning from the start frame's pose/state.
2. CAMERA MOVEMENT — how and when the camera moves, and how the subject looks AFTER the move. Plain camera language: slow dolly in, handheld follow, pan right, push in, circles around.
3. PHYSICAL PERFORMANCE — emotion through visible cues only (jaw tightens, eyes dart, shoulders drop), never abstract labels.
4. EVOLVING SCENE — only lighting/atmosphere CHANGES over the clip (a shadow passing, fog rolling in, a lamp flickering). Do not restate the static look already present in the frame.
5. AUDIO — ambient sound, music, and any speech. Put spoken lines in quotation marks; specify language/accent if relevant; describe voice quality.

## DIALOGUE
- Break long lines into short phrases with acting directions between each beat.
  Example pattern: A man speaks slowly, "I remember..." He pauses, looks to the side, then continues with a cracking voice, "...you never knew."
- Direct pacing, pauses, and physical acting per beat rather than describing a mood.

## HARD RULES — strip or rewrite
- NO internal-state labels (sad, confused, happy). Convert each to a visible physical cue.
- NO readable on-screen text or logos as a focal request — LTX does not render text reliably.
- NO complex/chaotic physics (shattering, swarming, intricate collisions). Normal human motion and dancing are fine.
- NO overloaded scenes. Keep one clear focal action.
- NO conflicting directions or mixed lighting logic. Stay internally consistent — and consistent with the frame(s).
- NO numerical micro-specs ("pan 2°/sec"). Use natural language.
- Do NOT narrate the static frame back. If a sentence only restates what's already visible, cut it.

[USER]
TARGET DURATION: {{seconds}}s
START FRAME (vision-model description of the input image): {{start_frame_description}}
LAST FRAME (optional vision-model description of the last frame, used only as a constraint; empty if none): {{last_frame_description}}
User idea / desired motion: {{original_prompt}}

nodes.title: Prompt
widgets_values: The cat looks with love at her human, stretches and then jump into her arms. Make the scene funny and cute

The image shows a young girl dressed in medieval armor, standing in a field with a castle in the background. She is wearing a helmet with a pom-pom on top and has a sword in her right hand. The girl is standing with her left hand on her hip and her right arm resting on her thigh. In front of her, there is a orange tabby cat sitting on the grass. The cat is looking up at the girl with a curious expression. The field is surrounded by trees and bushes, and there are mountains in the distance. The sky is blue and the overall mood of the image is peaceful and serene.

She is standing in a lush green landscape with a castle in the background. The cat is orange and is looking up at the girl with a curious expression.
